items.front: Under 2-5-1, how are the seven route types described?
items.back: a. VOR/VORTAC/TACAN airways — 'Victor' + number (low) or 'J' + number (jet). b. Alternate airways — 'Victor' + number + direction (e.g., Victor Twelve South). c. Colored/L/MF — color + number (e.g., Blue Eighty-One). d. Named routes — 'North American Route'/'Bahama Route' + number. e. ATS routes — phonetic letter(s) + number in group form. f. MTRs — 'I-R'/'V-R' + number. g. RNAV — high: 'Q' + number; low: phonetic letter + number.

items.front: Under 2-5-1, what is the difference between describing high and low altitude RNAV routes?
items.back: High altitude: letter 'Q' + route number in group form (e.g., 'Q One Forty-five'). Low altitude: phonetic letter + number in group form (e.g., 'Tango Two Ten'). The letter is NOT spoken phonetically for high altitude routes.

items.front: Under 2-5-2, what are the six methods for describing NAVAID-based positions?
items.back: 1. VOR/VORTAC/TACAN/GPS Waypoint radial — name + digits of radial + 'radial' (omit 'degrees'). 2. Arc — distance + 'mile arc' + compass direction + 'of' + NAVAID name. 3. Quadrant — NE/SE/SW/NW + distance in miles from NAVAID. 4. NDB — course/bearing + 'course to'/'bearing from' + name + 'radio beacon'. 5. MLS — azimuth + 'azimuth to'/'azimuth from' + name + 'MLS'. 6. NRS waypoint — FIR letter + subset letter + latitude increment + longitude increment.

items.front: Under 2-5-2a, how is a NAVAID described differently when used in a routing vs. as a clearance limit?
items.back: In routing: state name or phonetic alphabet equivalent (location identifier), e.g., 'V6 Victor Whiskey Victor (Waterville) V45 Jackson'. As clearance limit: state name of NAVAID followed by type of NAVAID if known, e.g., 'Cleared to Grand Rapids VOR'.

items.front: Under 2-5-2b, when stating a radial, what word is explicitly omitted?
items.back: The word 'degrees.' State separate digits of the radial/azimuth/bearing followed by 'radial/azimuth/bearing' — e.g., 'Appleton Zero Five Zero Radial' (not 'degrees radial').

items.front: Under 2-5-3, contrast how named vs. unnamed fixes are described.
items.back: NAMED (charted on SID/STAR/en route chart/approach plate): state the fix name. UNNAMED: state NAVAID name + radial/localizer/azimuth + distance in miles + 'mile fix'. Prohibited expressions: 'passing Victor Twelve', 'passing J Eleven'.

items.front: Under 2-5-3c, what two expressions are explicitly prohibited when describing a fix?
items.back: 'Passing Victor Twelve' and 'passing J Eleven.' Controllers must use specific terms (fix names or NAVAID radial/distance descriptions), not airway passing references.
